title: Plugin::load_legacy_manifest defaults silently fabricate plugin metadata from raw JSON
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/plugins/mod.rs:321-380
## Violation
Every field is extracted with `.as_str().unwrap_or("unknown")` or `.map(String::from)`. A legacy manifest with a missing/typo'd name silently loads as "unknown". Quoted lines:

    let name = legacy["name"].as_str().unwrap_or("unknown").to_string();
    ...
    let server_name = server["name"].as_str().unwrap_or("unknown").to_string();
    let transport = server["transport"].as_str().unwrap_or("stdio").to_string();
    map.insert(
        server_name,
        McpServerConfig {
            command: server["command"].as_str().map(String::from),

If two legacy plugins drop their `name`, both load with `id = "unknown"` and the second clobbers the first in the manager's HashMap. Same for "unknown" MCP servers. The "unknown" sentinel is a stringly-typed sentinel — a classic defensive-programming smell that hides upstream input bugs.
## Mandated refactor
Replace each `.unwrap_or("unknown")` with `.ok_or_else(|| PluginError::InvalidManifest("legacy manifest missing required field: name"))?`. Surface InvalidManifest to the manager so the broken plugin is reported, not silently shadowed. Add a dedicated `LegacyManifest` typed struct deserialised with serde + #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] so typos surface explicitly.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
